sprint: Platform Hardening — Draft Asset Architecture, Phase C: Creator/Studio rewiring
task: Rewires every real upload/read call site in root Studio (Creator) to go through the Phase A foundation (js/assetStore.js), the same fix Phase B already shipped for World Builder v2 -- closing the other half of the confirmed data-loss architecture bug (embedded base64 in a single, small-quota localStorage key). Two shared _storeUploadedAsset() helpers (js/app.js's own, duplicated into js/contextPanel.js/js/pageDesigner.js matching this codebase's established 'kept in lockstep by hand' precedent for small per-module adapters) now sit in front of every real write point: js/app.js's two insert paths (_insertPreparedSlide, the bulk multi-file upload.onchange loop), js/contextPanel.js's _applyImageResult (Place-artwork Add/Replace/Crop-Rotate, Place 1 and every extra Place alike) and _appendWorldObjectEditControl's image branch (a World-owned Scene Object's own Replace Image control, via SceneEngine.setContentOverride), and js/pageDesigner.js's Cover/Hook/End image-manager path (_applyPreparedPicture plus its defensive direct-load fallback) -- each downscales a large image first (AssetStore.downscaleImageDataURL, the exact 1.5MB/1600px/PNG-stays-PNG-else-JPEG-q0.85 algorithm World Builder v2's own private copy already used, duplicated into assetStore.js as one shared, Promise-based canonical version -- World Builder's own copy is completely untouched) then calls AssetStore.put(), handing whatever setter would have received the raw data: URI a small, durable vihu-asset: reference instead. ProjectManager.ensureProjectId() is newly exported (wrapping the pre-existing private _ensureProjectId, already used internally by the debounced save path) so an upload can get a stable project id even on a brand-new project's very first picture, before any save has ever run. ProjectManager.deserialize()'s rehydration (both Place 1's own image and every extra Place's own dataURL) now resolves a vihu-asset: reference via AssetStore.resolve() before ever handing it to loadImageFromDataURL, which only ever knew how to load a real URL -- a legacy data: URI passes through unchanged, same-tick, zero behaviour change for untouched old sessions; the stored _imageDataURL/placeContent[id].dataURL field itself is deliberately left holding the ORIGINAL reference string (never the resolved blob: URL), so the next save still writes the small, durable reference, not a transient per-tab URL. saveProjectAs() (the portable .vihu file export) is split per the plan's own §8: the ordinary local/cloud save path needs no change at all (it already just writes whatever small reference _imageDataURL holds), but a portable file must stay self-contained outside this app's own Supabase project -- a new _hydratePayloadForExport() walks every asset-bearing field in the just-built payload (pages[i].image, each Place's placeContent[*].dataURL, each World-owned object's elementOverrides[*].image) and calls AssetStore.hydrateForExport() on any still-vihu-asset: field, producing a real embedded data: URI in the downloaded file -- a per-field failure (offline, never cached) leaves that one field un-hydrated rather than aborting the whole export. Two real, previously-undiscovered read-side gaps were found and fixed at their own root, mirroring the exact pattern Phase B's own externalizeSceneImage fix already established: renderer/slideRenderer.js's _ensureDecorationImage/_layerDrawDecorationImage (a Story-Author-replaced World-owned object's own image override) and js/objectStrip.js's _renderThumb (that same object's Object Strip thumbnail) both used to assign an override's raw src directly as an /Image src -- now resolved through AssetStore.resolve() first (a placeholder-then-resolve pattern, cached under the original src key so a second call for a still-resolving src is a cache hit, not a duplicate resolve), with a legacy data:/http(s) URL still resolving through the same call, same-tick. js/pictureStudio.js's open() gained the matching fix for its own string-input branch -- Crop/Rotate re-editing an existing Place/Scene-Object picture whose cached Image object isn't yet warm now correctly resolves a vihu-asset: reference before assigning it as a real image src, instead of failing silently. js/themeEngine.js's own FileReader usage (confirmed via a repo-wide sweep of every readAsDataURL/FileReader call site, unchanged from Phase C's original 7-file scope) is Theme (.vtheme package) import, an unrelated concern, correctly left untouched. Verified via a new 20-assertion Playwright suite driving the REAL root Studio UI end to end (not internals): a real 2-file bulk upload through the actual #scanUpload input produces two distinct vihu-asset: references (not embedded base64, not accidentally deduped); the localStorage-persisted session genuinely shrinks to a small reference string; a real page reload (skipping the Traveller Gateway a second time, which plays on every boot including a reload) correctly restores the session with the picture rehydrated into a real, loaded Image, while _imageDataURL still holds the small reference, never a transient blob: URL; a real saveProjectAs() download is intercepted and its JSON inspected -- the exported file's page.image is confirmed a real, non-trivial embedded data: URI, not a reference, and the exported+reimported file round-trips with the same slide count; a synthetic AssetStore.put()/resolve() round trip confirms the read-side fix's own underlying mechanism. Both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2) and the existing Phase A/Phase B Playwright suites all pass unchanged, zero console errors beyond the three already-disclosed, pre-existing, unrelated Story Egg asset gaps (hero.png/magic.png/personality.json) this codebase's own history has already recorded. No cache-bust bump was needed -- root index.html's own script tags carry no ?v= convention (only its stylesheet link does, untouched since no CSS changed this phase). Confined to js/assetStore.js (additive downscale helper), js/projectManager.js, js/app.js, js/contextPanel.js, js/pageDesigner.js, js/pictureStudio.js, js/objectStrip.js, and renderer/slideRenderer.js. Phase D (migration activation -- wiring migrateFieldsOnSave into the debounced save paths so already-saved embedded-base64 content starts shrinking one save at a time) and Phase E (full end-to-end verification, docs, cleanup) remain their own separately-scoped, not-yet-started increments.
